title: Obsidian as a Second Brain for Claude
subtitle: A ClaudeTio guide
narration: Welcome. This is a quick guide to turning Obsidian into a second brain for Claude, so your notes and project context survive across every conversation.

title: Why Obsidian + Claude
items: Human and AI edit the same notes
narration: An Obsidian vault is just a folder of plain markdown files. That makes it a perfect long-term memory for Claude: no lock-in, fully searchable, and linkable with wikilinks.

title: Two ways to connect
colA_body:
Filesystem access

Simplest setup
Claude reads/writes vault files directly
colB_body:
Local REST API + MCP server

Structured search
Tag & frontmatter queries
narration: There are two ways to connect them. Option A, filesystem access, is the simplest: Claude just reads and writes files in your vault folder. Option B adds the Local REST API plugin and the M C P dash obsidian server, for structured search and tag queries.

title: Step 1: Vault structure
narration: Start by giving your vault a simple structure. This repo includes a setup script that creates the PARA folders: inbox, projects, areas, resources, and archive, plus a daily notes folder and templates.

title: Run the setup script
narration: Run setup-vault dot s h and point it at your vault path. It creates every folder and copies in the note templates automatically.

title: Option A: Filesystem access
narration: For Claude Code, just tell it where your vault lives, it already reads and writes files directly. For Claude Desktop, add the filesystem M C P server to your config, pointed at your vault path.

title: Option B: Local REST API + MCP
items: Install the "Local REST API" community plugin
Add mcp-obsidian to your Claude config
narration: For richer search, install the Local REST API community plugin inside Obsidian, copy its API key, and add the M C P dash obsidian server to your Claude config with that key.

title: Verify the connection
items: List the folders in my Obsidian vault.
Create a note in 00-Inbox called test-note.md.
Search my vault for notes tagged #project.
narration: Restart Claude, then verify the connection. Ask it to list your vault folders, create a test note, or search notes by tag.

title: Suggested workflow
items: Log session summaries in the daily note
Link related notes instead of repeating context
Agree on a small, consistent tag vocabulary
narration: For day to day use: capture fast into the inbox, ask Claude to log session summaries into today's daily note, and link related notes instead of repeating context.

title: Get started
subtitle: github.com/Tiobold/ClaudeTio
narration: Everything shown here, the setup script, the templates, and the example config, is in the ClaudeTio repo. Check the README to get started.
